This is documentation for Tabsdata 1.9.0, which is no longer actively maintained.
For up-to-date documentation, see the latest version (2.0.0).
  • By Module
  • tabsdata.tableframe.expr.expr
Version: 1.9.0

tabsdata.tableframe.expr.expr

Expr
class Expr(
    expr: Expr | Expr,
)

Bases: object

abs​

def abs() -> Expr

Return the abso lute value of the expression.

Categories: numeric

add​

def add(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Equivalent to the + operator.

For numeric types adds the given input. For string types concatenates the given input.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to add or concatenate.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").add(1).alias("add"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ add      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆ 2        │
│ 15   ┆ 16       │
│ 18   ┆ 19       │
│ 60   ┆ 61       │
│ 60   ┆ 61       │
│ 75   ┆ 76       │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

alias​

def alias(
    name: str,
) -> Expr

Set the name for a column or expression.

Categories: manipulation

Parameters:

namestrrequired

Column or expression new name. The name must be a word ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*) of up to 100 characters.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").alias("Age"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ age  ┆ Age  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 15   │
│ 18   ┆ 18   │
│ 60   ┆ 60   │
│ 60   ┆ 60   │
│ 75   ┆ 75   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

and_​

def and_(
    * others: Any,
) -> Expr

Bitwise and operator with the given expressions. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

othersrequired

expressions to peform the bitwiseand with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("i"), td.col("i").and_(2).alias("and_"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ i    ┆ and_ │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1   ┆ 2    │
│ 2    ┆ 2    │
│ -3   ┆ 0    │
│ 0    ┆ 0    │
│ 5    ┆ 0    │
│ 7    ┆ 2    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

arccos​

def arccos() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse cosine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arccos().alias("arccos"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arccos   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 1.560796 │
│ 0.15 ┆ 1.420228 │
│ 0.18 ┆ 1.38981  │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.927295 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.927295 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.722734 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

arccosh​

def arccosh() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic cosine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arccosh().alias("arccosh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arccosh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ NaN      │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.962424 │
│ 1.8  ┆ 1.192911 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.477889 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.477889 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 2.703576 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

arcsin​

def arcsin() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse sine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arcsin().alias("arcsin"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arcsin   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.150568 │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.180986 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.643501 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.643501 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.848062 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

arcsinh​

def arcsinh() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic sine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arcsinh().alias("arcsinh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arcsinh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 0.099834 │
│ 1.5  ┆ 1.194763 │
│ 1.8  ┆ 1.350441 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.49178  │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.49178  │
│ 7.5  ┆ 2.712465 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

arctan​

def arctan() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse tangent of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), , td.col("val").arctan().alias("arctan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arctan   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.14889  │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.178093 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.54042  │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.54042  │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.643501 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

arctanh​

def arctanh() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic tangent of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), , td.col("val").arctanh().alias("arctanh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arctanh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.15114  │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.181983 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.693147 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.693147 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.972955 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

cast​

def cast(
    dtype: Boolean | Categorical | Date | Datetime | Decimal | Duration | Enum | Float16 | Float32 | Float64 | Int8 | Int16 | Int64 | Int32 | Int128 | Null | String | Time | UInt8 | UInt16 | UInt32 | UInt64 | type [ Any ],
    *,
    strict: bool = True,
    wrap_numerical: bool = False,
) -> Expr

Cast a value to d different type.

Categories: type_casting

Parameters:

dtypeBoolean | Categorical | Date | Datetime | Decimal | Duration | Enum | Float16 | Float32 | Float64 | Int8 | Int16 | Int64 | Int32 | Int128 | Null | String | Time | UInt8 | UInt16 | UInt32 | UInt64 | type [ Any ]required

The data type to cast to.

strictbooldefault: True

If false, invalid casts produce null’s; if true, an excetion is raised.

wrap_numericalbooldefault: False

If true, overflowing numbers ara handled; if false, an excetion is raised.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>> import polars as pl
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cast(td.Float64)).alias("cast")
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ cast │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1.0  │
│ 15   ┆ 15.0 │
│ 18   ┆ 18.0 │
│ 60   ┆ 60.0 │
│ 60   ┆ 60.0 │
│ 75   ┆ 75.0 │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

cbrt​

def cbrt() -> Expr

Calculate the cub root of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cbrt().alias("cbrt"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ cbrt     │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1.0      │
│ 15   ┆ 2.466212 │
│ 18   ┆ 2.620741 │
│ 60   ┆ 3.914868 │
│ 60   ┆ 3.914868 │
│ 75   ┆ 4.217163 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

ceil​

def ceil() -> Expr

Round up the expression to the next integer value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").first().ceil().alias("ceil"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ ceil    │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ f64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  1.0    │
│ 1.1  ┆  2.0    │
└──────┴─────────┘

clip​

def clip(
    lower_bound: int | float | Decimal | date | time | datetime | timedelta | Expr | Series | str | None = None,
    upper_bound: int | float | Decimal | date | time | datetime | timedelta | Expr | Series | str | None = None,
) -> Expr

For element values outside the lower and upper bounds, lower values are replaced with the lower bound and upper values with the upper bound. Values within the lower and upper bounds are unaffected.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

lower_boundint | float | Decimal | date | time | datetime | timedelta | Expr | Series | str | Nonedefault: None

The lower bound value. If None, the lower bound is not set.

upper_boundint | float | Decimal | date | time | datetime | timedelta | Expr | Series | str | Nonedefault: None

The upper bound value. If None, the upper bound is

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").clip(18,65).alias("clip"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ age  ┆ clip    │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═══+++═══╡
│ 1    ┆ 18      │
│ 18   ┆ 18      │
│ 50   ┆ 50      │
│ 65   ┆ 65      │
│ 70   ┆ 65      │
└──────┴─────────┘

cos​

def cos() -> Expr

Calculate the cosine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cos().alias("cos"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ val  ┆ cos       │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64  ┆ f64       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 0.995004  │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.070737  │
│ 1.8  ┆ -0.227202 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 0.96017   │
│ 6.0  ┆ 0.96017   │
│ 7.5  ┆ 0.346635  │
│ null ┆ null      │
└──────┴───────────┘

cosh​

def cosh() -> Expr

Calculate the hyperbolic cosine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cosh().alias("cosh"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ val  ┆ cosh       │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ f64  ┆ f64        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 1.005004   │
│ 1.5  ┆ 2.35241    │
│ 1.8  ┆ 3.107473   │
│ 6.0  ┆ 201.715636 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 201.715636 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 904.021484 │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘

cot​

def cot() -> Expr

Calculate the cotangent of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").cot().alias("cot"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ val  ┆ cot       │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64  ┆ f64       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 9.966644  │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.070915  │
│ 1.8  ┆ -0.233304 │
│ 6.0  ┆ -3.436353 │
│ 6.0  ┆ -3.436353 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 0.369547  │
│ null ┆ null      │
└──────┴───────────┘

count​

def count() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that counts the non null values of the given column in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 2    │
│ A    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ C    ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ 6    │
│ C    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).count().alias("count"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ a    ┆ count │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ str  ┆ u32   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ null ┆ 1     │
│ A    ┆ 2     │
│ B    ┆ 2     │
│ C    ┆ 1     │
└──────┴───────┘

degrees​

def degrees() -> Expr

Convert a radian value to degrees

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").degrees().alias("degrees"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────────┐
│ val  ┆ degress    │
│ ---  ┆ ---        │
│ f64  ┆ f64        │
╞══════╪════════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 5.729578   │
│ 1.5  ┆ 85.943669  │
│ 1.8  ┆ 103.132403 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 343.774677 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 343.774677 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 429.718346 │
│ null ┆ null       │
└──────┴────────────┘

diff​

def diff(
    n: int = 1,
) -> Expr

Compute the difference between an element value and the element value of the specified relative row.

It supports numberic and datetime types.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

nintdefault: 1

The relative row to compute the difference with. Defaults to 1 (previous). Use a negative number to get a next row.

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("i"), td.col("i").diff().diff().alias("diff"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ i    ┆ diff │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ null │
│ 0    ┆ -1   │
│ 2    ┆ 2    │
│ 3    ┆ 1    │
│ 4    ┆ 1    │
│ -1   ┆ -5   │
│ -2   ┆ -1   │
│ -3   ┆ -1   │
│ -4   ┆ -1   │
│ -5   ┆ -1   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

eq​

def eq(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Compare if 2 expressions are equal, equivalent to expr == other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns null.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").eq(td.col("b")).alias("eq"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ eq    │
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool  │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true  │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ true  │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ false │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ null  │
│ null┆ null ┆ null  │
└─────┴──────┴───────┘

eq_missing​

def eq_missing(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Compare if 2 expressions are equal an, equivalent to expr == other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns false.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").eq(td.col("b")).alias("eq_missing"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ eq_missing│
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool      │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false     │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true      │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ true      │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ false     │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ false     │
│ null┆ null ┆ true      │
└─────┴──────┴───────────┘

exp​

def exp() -> Expr

Calculate the exponential of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").exp().alias("exp"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────────┐
│ val  ┆ exp         │
│ ---  ┆ ---         │
│ f64  ┆ f64         │
╞══════╪═════════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ 1.105171    │
│ 6.0  ┆ 403.428793  │
│ 6.0  ┆ 403.428793  │
│ 7.5  ┆ 1808.042414 │
│ null ┆ null        │
└──────┴─────────────┘

fill_nan​

def fill_nan(
    value: int | float | Expr | None,
) -> Expr

Replace NaN values with the given value.

Categories: manipulation

Parameters:

valueint | float | Expr | Nonerequired

The value to replaceNaN values with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").fill_nan(5.5)
>>>        .alias("fill_nan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ fill_nan │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ 1.1      │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0      │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ 5.5      │
└──────┴──────────┘

fill_null​

def fill_null(
    value: Any | Expr | None = None,
    strategy: Literal [ 'forward' , 'backward' , 'min' , 'max' , 'mean' , 'zero' , 'one' ] | None = None,
    limit: int | None = None,
) -> Expr

Replace null values with the given value.

Categories: manipulation

Parameters:

valueAny | Expr | Nonedefault: None

The value to replacenull values with.

strategyLiteral [ 'forward' , 'backward' , 'min' , 'max' , 'mean' , 'zero' , 'one' ] | Nonedefault: None

The strategy to use for fillingnull values.

limitint | Nonedefault: None

The maximum number ofnull values to replace.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val")
>>>        .fill_null(5.5).alias("fill_null"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────────┐
│ val  ┆ fill_null │
│ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64  ┆ f64       │
╞══════╪═══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ -1.0      │
│ 0.0  ┆ 0.0       │
│ 1.1  ┆ 1.1       │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0       │
│ inf  ┆ inf       │
│ null ┆ 5.5       │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN       │
└──────┴───────────┘

filter​

def filter(
    * predicates: Expr | Series | str | Iterable [ Expr | Series | str ],
) -> Expr

Apply a filter predicate to an expression.

Elements for which the predicate does not evaluate to true are discarded, evaluations to null are also discarded.

Useful in an aggregation expression.

Categories: filters

Parameters:

predicatesrequired

Expression(s) that evaluates to a boolean.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌───────┬─────────┐
│ state ┆ tickets │
│ ---   ┆ ---     │
│ str   ┆ i64     │
╞═══════╪═════════╡
│ CA    ┆ 1       │
│ AL    ┆ 3       │
│ CA    ┆ 2       │
│ NY    ┆ 2       │
│ NY    ┆ 3       │
└───────┴─────────┘
>>>
>>> import tabsdata as td
>>> tf.group_by("state").agg(td.col("tickets")
>>>   .filter(td.col("tickets") !=2)
>>>   .sum().alias("sum_non_two"))
>>>
┌───────┬─────────────┐
│ state ┆ sum_non_two │
│ ---   ┆ ---         │
│ str   ┆ i64         │
╞═══════╪═════════════╡
│ AL    ┆ 3           │
│ NY    ┆ 3           │
│ CA    ┆ 1           │
└───────┴─────────────┘

first​

def first() -> Expr

Get the first element.

Categories: aggregation

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").first().alias("first"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ age  ┆ first   │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 10   ┆ 10      │
│ 11   ┆ 10      │
│ 18   ┆ 10      │
│ 65   ┆ 10      │
│ 70   ┆ 10      │
└──────┴─────────┘

floor​

def floor() -> Expr

Round down the expression to the previous integer value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").ceil().alias("floor"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ floor   │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  1      │
│ 1.1  ┆  1      │
└──────┴─────────┘

floordiv​

def floordiv(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Calculate the floor on the division.

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to divide by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").floordiv(2)
>>>        .alias("floordiv"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ floordiv │
│ ---  ┆ -------  │
│ f64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 2.5  ┆  1       │
│ 1.4  ┆  0       │
└──────┴──────────┘

ge​

def ge(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Greater or equal operator.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").ge(1.0).alias("ge"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ ge      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 0.9  ┆  false  │
│ 1.1  ┆  true   │
└──────┴─────────┘

gt​

def gt(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Greater than operator.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").gt(1.0).alias("gt"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ ge      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  false  │
│ 1.1  ┆  true   │
└──────┴─────────┘

hash​

def hash(
    seed: int = 0,
    seed_1: int | None = None,
    seed_2: int | None = None,
    seed_3: int | None = None,
) -> Expr

Compute the hash of an element value.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

seedintdefault: 0

The seed for the hash function.

seed_1int | Nonedefault: None

The first seed for the hash function.

seed_2int | Nonedefault: None

The second seed for the hash function.

seed_3int | Nonedefault: None

The third seed for the hash function.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").hash(5).alias("hash"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────────────────┐
│ temp ┆ hash                │
│ ---  ┆ ---                 │
│ f64  ┆ u64                 │
╞══════╪═════════════════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ 1438840365631752616 │
│ 2.0  ┆ 4738789230185236462 │
└──────┴─────────────────────┘

is_between​

def is_between(
    lower_bound: td_typing.IntoExpr,
    upper_bound: td_typing.IntoExpr,
    closed: td_typing.ClosedInterval = 'both',
) -> Expr

If an expression is between the given bounds.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

lower_boundtd_typing.IntoExprrequired

The lower bound value.

upper_boundtd_typing.IntoExprrequired

The upper bound value.

closedtd_typing.ClosedIntervaldefault: 'both'

The interval type, either “both”, “left”, “right”, or “neither”

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp")
>>> .is_between(0, 1).alias("between"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ between │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│-1.0  ┆  false  │
│ 0.0  ┆  true   │
│ 0.5  ┆  true   │
│ 1.0  ┆  true   │
│ 1.1  ┆  false  │
└──────┴─────────┘

is_finite​

def is_finite() -> Expr

If an element value is finite.

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_finite()
>>>        .alias("finite"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────┐
│ temp ┆ finite │
│ ---  ┆ ---    │
│ f64  ┆ bool   │
╞══════╪════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true   │
│ 2.0  ┆ true   │
│ inf  ┆ false  │
└──────┴────────┘

is_in​

def is_in(
    other: Expr | Collection [ Any ],
) -> Expr

If an element value is in the given collection.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherExpr | Collection [ Any ]required

The collection to check if the element value is in.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_in([1.1, 2.2])
>>>        .alias("is_in"))
>>>
┌──────┬────────┐
│ temp ┆ is_in  │
│ ---  ┆ ---    │
│ f64  ┆ bool   │
╞══════╪════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true   │
│ 2.0  ┆ false  │
│ inf  ┆ false  │
└──────┴────────┘

is_infinite​

def is_infinite() -> Expr

If an element value is infinite.

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp")
>>>        .is_infinite().alias("infinite"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ infinite │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ 2.0  ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘

is_nan​

def is_nan() -> Expr

If an element value is NaN.

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_nan().alias("nan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ nan      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ Nan  ┆ true     │
│ None ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ false    │
└──────┴──────────┘

is_not_nan​

def is_not_nan() -> Expr

If an element value is not NaN.

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_not_nan()
>>>        .alias("not_nan"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ not_nan  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true     │
│ Nan  ┆ false    │
│ None ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘

is_not_null​

def is_not_null() -> Expr

If an element value is not null (None).

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_not_null()
>>>        .alias("null"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ not_nulL │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ true     │
│ Nan  ┆ true     │
│ None ┆ false    │
│ inf  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘

is_null​

def is_null() -> Expr

If an element value is null (None).

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_not_null()
>>>        .alias("null"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ null     │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ Nan  ┆ false    │
│ None ┆ true     │
│ inf  ┆ false    │
└──────┴──────────┘

is_unique​

def is_unique() -> Expr

If an element value is unique for all values in the column.

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").is_unique()
>>>        .alias("unique"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ temp ┆ unique   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ bool     │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ 1.1  ┆ false    │
│ None ┆ true     │
│ 2.0  ┆ true     │
└──────┴──────────┘

last​

def last() -> Expr

Get the last element.

Categories: aggregation

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").first().alias("last"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ age  ┆ last    │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ i64  ┆ i64     │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 10   ┆ 70      │
│ 11   ┆ 70      │
│ 18   ┆ 70      │
│ 65   ┆ 70      │
│ 70   ┆ 70      │
└──────┴─────────┘

le​

def le(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Less or equal operator.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("temp"), td.col("temp").le(1.0).alias("le"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ temp ┆ le      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 0.9  ┆  true   │
│ 1.1  ┆  false  │
└──────┴─────────┘

len​

def len() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that counts the rows in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ a    ┆ b    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 2    │
│ A    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ C    ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ 6    │
│ C    ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).len().alias("len"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ a    ┆ len │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ u32 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ null ┆ 1   │
│ A    ┆ 2   │
│ B    ┆ 2   │
│ C    ┆ 2   │
└──────┴─────┘

log​

def log(
    base: float = 2.718281828459045,
) -> Expr

Calculate the logarithm to the given base.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

basefloatdefault: 2.718281828459045

logarithm base, defaults toe.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").log().alias("log")
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ log      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ NaN      │
│ 0.0  ┆ -inf     │
│ 1.1  ┆ 0.09531  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 0.693147 │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┘

log10​

def log10() -> Expr

Calculate the logarithm base 10.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").log10().alias("log10")
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ log10    │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ NaN      │
│ 0.0  ┆ -inf     │
│ 1.1  ┆ 0.041393 │
│ 2.0  ┆ 0.30103  │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┘

log1p​

def log1p() -> Expr

Calculate the natural logarithm plus one.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").log1p().alias("log1p")
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ log1p    │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ -inf     │
│ 0.0  ┆ 0.0      │
│ 1.1  ┆ 0.741937 │
│ 2.0  ┆ 1.098612 │
│ inf  ┆ inf      │
│ null ┆ null     │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
└──────┴──────────┘

lt​

def lt(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Less than operator.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").lt(1.0).alias("lt"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────┐
│ val  ┆ tl      │
│ ---  ┆ ------- │
│ f64  ┆ bool    │
╞══════╪═════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆  false  │
│ 0.1  ┆  true   │
└──────┴─────────┘

max​

def max() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that finds the maximum value in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).max())
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

mean​

def mean() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that finds the mean of the values in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b").mean())
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ ss   ┆ i        │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ str  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ null ┆ null     │
│ A    ┆ 1.5      │
│ F    ┆ -5.0     │
│ C    ┆ -2.0     │
│ D    ┆ -4.0     │
│ B    ┆ 2.333333 │
└──────┴──────────┘

median​

def median() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that finds the median of the values in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).median())
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ F    ┆ -5.0 │
│ C    ┆ -2.0 │
│ B    ┆ 3.0  │
│ D    ┆ -4.0 │
│ A    ┆ 1.5  │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

min​

def min() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that finds the minimum value in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).min())
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ null ┆ null │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
└──────┴──────┘

mod​

def mod(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Modulus operator.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to divide by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").mod(5).alias("mod"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ mod  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 0    │
│ 18   ┆ 3    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

mul​

def mul(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Multiplication operator.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to multiply by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").mul(10).alias("mul"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ mul  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 10   │
│ 15   ┆ 150  │
│ 18   ┆ 180  │
│ 75   ┆ 750  │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

n_unique​

def n_unique() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that counts the unique values of the given column in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).n_unique())
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ ss   ┆ i   │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ u32 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ D    ┆ 1   │
│ C    ┆ 3   │
│ A    ┆ 2   │
│ B    ┆ 3   │
│ F    ┆ 1   │
│ null ┆ 1   │
└──────┴─────┘

ne​

def ne(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Compare if 2 expressions are not equal, equivalent to expr != other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns null.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").ne(td.col("b")).alias("ne"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ ne    │
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool  │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true  │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ false │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ true  │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ null  │
│ null┆ null ┆ null  │
└─────┴──────┴───────┘

ne_missing​

def ne_missing(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Compare if 2 expressions are not equal an, equivalent to expr != other. If one of the expressions is null (None) it returns false.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to compare with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("a").eq(td.col("b")).alias("ne_missing"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────┬───────────┐
│ a   ┆ b    ┆ ne_missing│
│ --- ┆ ---  ┆ ---       │
│ f64 ┆ f64  ┆ bool      │
╞═════╪══════╪═══════════╡
│ 1.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ true      │
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0  ┆ false     │
│ NaN ┆ NaN  ┆ false     │
│ 4.0 ┆ NaN  ┆ true      │
│ 5.0 ┆ null ┆ true      │
│ null┆ null ┆ false     │
└─────┴──────┴───────────┘

neg​

def neg() -> Expr

Unary minus operator.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").neg().alias("neg"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ neg  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ 1.0  │
│ 0.0  ┆ -0.0 │
│ 1.1  ┆ -1.1 │
│ 2.0  ┆ -2.0 │
│ inf  ┆ -inf │
│ null ┆ null │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
└──────┴──────┘

not_​

def not_() -> Expr

Negate a boolean expression.

Categories: logic

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").not_().alias("not"))
>>>
┌───────┬───────┐
│ val   ┆ not   │
│ ---   ┆ ---   │
│ bool  ┆ bool  │
╞═══════╪═══════╡
│ true  ┆ false │
│ false ┆ true  │
│ null  ┆ null  │
└───────┴───────┘

or_​

def or_(
    * others: Any,
) -> Expr

Bitwise or operator with the given expressions. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

othersrequired

expressions to peform the bitwiseor with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").or_(1).alias("or"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ or   │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 15   │
│ 18   ┆ 19   │
│ 60   ┆ 61   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

pow​

def pow(
    exponent: Expr | Series | str | int | float,
) -> Expr

Exponentiation operator.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

exponentExpr | Series | str | int | floatrequired

exponent value.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").pow(2).alias("pow"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ age  ┆ pow  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 225  │
│ 18   ┆ 324  │
│ 60   ┆ 3600 │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘

radians​

def radians() -> Expr

Convert a degree value to radians

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").radians().alias("radians"))
>>>
┌─────────┬──────────┐
│ val     ┆ radians  │
│ ---     ┆ ---      │
│ i64     ┆ f64      │
╞═════════╪══════════╡
│ 1       ┆ 0.017453 │
│ 15      ┆ 0.261799 │
│ 60      ┆ 1.047198 │
│ 75      ┆ 1.308997 │
│ null    ┆ null     │
└─────────┴──────────┘

rank​

def rank(
    method: Literal [ 'average' , 'min' , 'max' , 'dense' , 'ordinal' , 'random' ] = 'average',
    *,
    descending: bool = False,
    seed: int | None = None,
) -> Expr

Compute the rank of the element values. Multiple rank types are available.

Categories: aggregation

Parameters:

methodLiteral [ 'average' , 'min' , 'max' , 'dense' , 'ordinal' , 'random' ]default: 'average'

the ranking type: ‘average’ (default), ‘dense’, ‘max’, ‘min’, ‘ordinal’ or ‘random’.

descendingbooldefault: False

if the order is ascending (default) or descending.

seedint | Nonedefault: None

random seed when using ‘random’ rank type.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").rank("max").alias("rank"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ val  ┆ rank │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ u32  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ 1    │
│ 0.0  ┆ 2    │
│ 1.1  ┆ 3    │
│ 2.0  ┆ 4    │
│ inf  ┆ 5    │
│ null ┆ null │
│ NaN  ┆ 6    │
└──────┴──────┘

reinterpret​

def reinterpret(
    *,
    signed: bool = True,
) -> Expr

Reinterpret the 64bit element values (i64 or u64) as a signed/unsigned integers. Only valid for 64bit integers, for other types use cast.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

signedbooldefault: True

true to convert to i64, false to convert to u64. This named argument must be specified.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val")
>>>   .reinterpret(signed=False).alias("reinterpret"))
>>>
┌──────┬─────────────┐
│ val  ┆ reinterpret │
│ ---  ┆ ---         │
│ i64  ┆ u64         │
╞══════╪═════════════╡
│ 3    ┆ 3           │
│ 1    ┆ 1           │
│ 5    ┆ 5           │
│ 4    ┆ 4           │
│ 2    ┆ 2           │
│ 6    ┆ 6           │
│ null ┆ null        │
└──────┴─────────────┘

round​

def round(
    decimals: int = 0,
) -> Expr

Round floating point element values.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

decimalsintdefault: 0

number of decimal places to round to.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").round().alias("round"))
>>>
┌──────┬───────┐
│ val  ┆ round │
│ ---  ┆ ---   │
│ f64  ┆ f64   │
╞══════╪═══════╡
│ -1.0 ┆ -1.0  │
│ 0.0  ┆ 0.0   │
│ 1.1  ┆ 1.0   │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0   │
│ inf  ┆ inf   │
│ null ┆ null  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN   │
└──────┴───────┘

round_sig_figs​

def round_sig_figs(
    digits: int,
) -> Expr

Round floating point element values to the specified significant figures.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

digitsintrequired

number of digits to round up.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").round_sig_figs(2)
>>>   .alias("round_sig_figs"))
>>>
┌────────┬────────────────┐
│ val    ┆ round_sig_figs │
│ ---    ┆ ---            │
│ f64    ┆ f64            │
╞════════╪════════════════╡
│ 0.0123 ┆ 0.012          │
│ 2.0244 ┆ 2.0            │
│ 0.0    ┆ 0.0            │
│ inf    ┆ NaN            │
│ 50.0   ┆ 50.0           │
│ 1.0    ┆ 1.0            │
│ NaN    ┆ NaN            │
│ null   ┆ null           │
│ 112.0  ┆ 110.0          │
│ 2142.0 ┆ 2100.0         │
└────────┴────────────────┘

shrink_dtype​

def shrink_dtype() -> Expr

Cast down a column to the smallest type that can hold the element values.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").shrink_dtype()
>>>   .alias("shrink_dtype"))
>>>
┌───────┬──────────────┐
│ val   ┆ shrink_dtype │
│ ---   ┆ ---          │
│ i64   ┆ i32          │
╞═══════╪══════════════╡
│ 0     ┆ 0            │
│ 256   ┆ 256          │
│ 65025 ┆ 65025        │
└───────┴──────────────┘

sign​

def sign() -> Expr

Calculate the sign of element values.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sign().alias("sign"))
>>>
┌────────┬──────┐
│ val    ┆ sign │
│ ---    ┆ ---  │
│ f64    ┆ f64  │
╞════════╪══════╡
│ 0.0123 ┆ 1.0  │
│ 2.0244 ┆ 1.0  │
│ 0.0    ┆ 0.0  │
│ inf    ┆ 1.0  │
│ -50.0  ┆ -1.0 │
│ 1.0    ┆ 1.0  │
│ NaN    ┆ NaN  │
│ null   ┆ null │
│ -112.0 ┆ -1.0 │
│ 2142.0 ┆ 1.0  │
└────────┴──────┘

sin​

def sin() -> Expr

Calculate the sine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sin().alias("sin"))
>>>
┌─────┬───────────┐
│ val ┆ sin       │
│ --- ┆ ---       │
│ i64 ┆ f64       │
╞═════╪═══════════╡
│ 0   ┆ 0.0       │
│ 30  ┆ -0.988032 │
│ 60  ┆ -0.304811 │
│ 90  ┆ 0.893997  │
└─────┴───────────┘

sinh​

def sinh() -> Expr

Calculate the hyperbolic sine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sinh().alias("sinh"))
>>>
┌─────┬───────────┐
│ val ┆ sinh      │
│ --- ┆ ---       │
│ i64 ┆ f64       │
╞═════╪═══════════╡
│ 0   ┆ 0.0       │
│ 30  ┆ 5.3432e12 │
│ 60  ┆ 5.7100e25 │
│ 90  ┆ 6.1020e38 │
└─────┴───────────┘

slice​

def slice(
    offset: int | Expr,
    length: int | Expr | None = None,
) -> Expr

Compute a slice of the TableFrame for the specified columns.

Categories: filters

Parameters:

offsetint | Exprrequired

the offset to start the slice.

lengthint | Expr | Nonedefault: None

the length of the slice.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ x    ┆ y    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ f64  ┆ f64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ 2.0  ┆ 2.0  │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN  │
│ 4.0  ┆ NaN  │
│ 5.0  ┆ null │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.select(tf.all().slice(1,2))
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ x   ┆ y   │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ f64 ┆ f64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ 2.0 ┆ 2.0 │
│ NaN ┆ NaN │
└─────┴─────┘

sqrt​

def sqrt() -> Expr

Calculate the square root of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sqrt().alias("sqrt"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ sqrt     │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1.0  ┆ 1.0      │
│ 2.0  ┆ 1.414214 │
│ NaN  ┆ NaN      │
│ 4.0  ┆ 2.0      │
│ 5.0  ┆ 2.236068 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

sub​

def sub(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Equivalent to the - operator.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

value to subtract.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").sub(1).alias("sub"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ sub      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆  0       │
│ 15   ┆ 14       │
│ 18   ┆ 17       │
│ 60   ┆ 59       │
│ 60   ┆ 59       │
│ 75   ┆ 74       │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘

sum​

def sum() -> Expr

Aggregation operation that sums the values in the group.

Categories: aggregation

Examples​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ ss   ┆ i    │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ str  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ A    ┆ 1    │
│ B    ┆ 0    │
│ A    ┆ 2    │
│ B    ┆ 3    │
│ B    ┆ 4    │
│ C    ┆ -1   │
│ C    ┆ -2   │
│ C    ┆ -3   │
│ D    ┆ -4   │
│ F    ┆ -5   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
>>>
>>> tf.group_by(td.col("a")).agg(td.col("b")).sum())
>>>
┌──────┬─────┐
│ ss   ┆ i   │
│ ---  ┆ --- │
│ str  ┆ i64 │
╞══════╪═════╡
│ null ┆ 0   │
│ A    ┆ 3   │
│ B    ┆ 7   │
│ C    ┆ -6  │
│ D    ┆ -4  │
│ F    ┆ -5  │
└──────┴─────┘

tan​

def tan() -> Expr

Calculate the tangent of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").tan().alias("tan"))
>>>
┌─────┬───────────┐
│ val ┆ tan       │
│ --- ┆ ---       │
│ i64 ┆ f64       │
╞═════╪═══════════╡
│ 0   ┆ 0.0       │
│ 30  ┆ -6.405331 │
│ 60  ┆ 0.32004   │
│ 90  ┆ -1.9952   │
└─────┴───────────┘

tanh​

def tanh() -> Expr

Calculate the hyperbolic tangent of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").tanh().alias("tanh"))
>>>
┌─────┬──────────┐
│ val ┆ tanh     │
│ --- ┆ ---      │
│ f64 ┆ f64      │
╞═════╪══════════╡
│ 0.0 ┆ 0.0      │
│ 3.0 ┆ 0.995055 │
│ 6.0 ┆ 0.999988 │
│ 9.0 ┆ 1.0      │
└─────┴──────────┘

truediv​

def truediv(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Equivalent to the float / operator.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

value to divide by.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").truediv(3).alias("truediv"))
>>>
┌────────┬────────────┐
│ val    ┆ truediv    │
│ ---    ┆ ---        │
│ f64    ┆ f64        │
╞════════╪════════════╡
│ 0.0123 ┆ 0.0041     │
│ 2.0244 ┆ 0.6748     │
│ 0.0    ┆ 0.0        │
│ inf    ┆ inf        │
│ -50.0  ┆ -16.666667 │
│ 1.0    ┆ 0.333333   │
│ NaN    ┆ NaN        │
│ null   ┆ null       │
│ -112.0 ┆ -37.333333 │
│ 2142.0 ┆ 714.0      │
└────────┴────────────┘

xor​

def xor(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Bitwise xor operator with the given expression. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

expression to peform the bitwisexor with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").xor(8).alias("xor"))
>>>
┌─────┬─────┐
│ val ┆ xor │
│ --- ┆ --- │
│ i64 ┆ i64 │
╞═════╪═════╡
│ 0   ┆ 8   │
│ 30  ┆ 22  │
│ 60  ┆ 52  │
│ 90  ┆ 82  │
└─────┴─────┘
dtExprDateTimeNameSpace

type_casting

Return an object namespace with all date-time methods for a date-time value.

Type: categories

strExprStringNameSpace

type_casting

Return an object namespace with all string methods for a string value.

Type: categories

abs
def abs() -> Expr

Return the abso lute value of the expression.

Categories: numeric

add
def add(
    other: Any,
) -> Expr

Equivalent to the + operator.

For numeric types adds the given input. For string types concatenates the given input.

Categories: numeric

Parameters:

otherAnyrequired

The value to add or concatenate.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").add(1).alias("add"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ add      │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ i64  ┆ i64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 1    ┆ 2        │
│ 15   ┆ 16       │
│ 18   ┆ 19       │
│ 60   ┆ 61       │
│ 60   ┆ 61       │
│ 75   ┆ 76       │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
alias
def alias(
    name: str,
) -> Expr

Set the name for a column or expression.

Categories: manipulation

Parameters:

namestrrequired

Column or expression new name. The name must be a word ([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*) of up to 100 characters.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("age"), td.col("age").alias("Age"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ age  ┆ Age  │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ 1    ┆ 1    │
│ 15   ┆ 15   │
│ 18   ┆ 18   │
│ 60   ┆ 60   │
│ 60   ┆ 60   │
│ 75   ┆ 75   │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
and_
def and_(
    * others: Any,
) -> Expr

Bitwise and operator with the given expressions. It can be used with integer and bool types.

Categories: logic

Parameters:

othersrequired

expressions to peform the bitwiseand with.

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf.select(td.col("i"), td.col("i").and_(2).alias("and_"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────┐
│ i    ┆ and_ │
│ ---  ┆ ---  │
│ i64  ┆ i64  │
╞══════╪══════╡
│ -1   ┆ 2    │
│ 2    ┆ 2    │
│ -3   ┆ 0    │
│ 0    ┆ 0    │
│ 5    ┆ 0    │
│ 7    ┆ 2    │
│ null ┆ null │
└──────┴──────┘
arccos
def arccos() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse cosine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arccos().alias("arccos"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arccos   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 1.560796 │
│ 0.15 ┆ 1.420228 │
│ 0.18 ┆ 1.38981  │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.927295 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.927295 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.722734 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arccosh
def arccosh() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic cosine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arccosh().alias("arccosh"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arccosh  │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.1  ┆ NaN      │
│ 1.5  ┆ 0.962424 │
│ 1.8  ┆ 1.192911 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.477889 │
│ 6.0  ┆ 2.477889 │
│ 7.5  ┆ 2.703576 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arcsin
def arcsin() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse sine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arcsin().alias("arcsin"))
>>>
┌──────┬──────────┐
│ val  ┆ arcsin   │
│ ---  ┆ ---      │
│ f64  ┆ f64      │
╞══════╪══════════╡
│ 0.01 ┆ 0.01     │
│ 0.15 ┆ 0.150568 │
│ 0.18 ┆ 0.180986 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.643501 │
│ 0.6  ┆ 0.643501 │
│ 0.75 ┆ 0.848062 │
│ null ┆ null     │
└──────┴──────────┘
arcsinh
def arcsinh() -> Expr

Calculate the inverse hyperbolic sine of the element value.

Categories: numeric

Example​

>>> import tabsdata as td
>>>
>>> tf: td.TableFrame ...
>>>
>>> tf = tf.select(td.col("val"), td.col("val").arcsinh().alias("arcsinh"))
